stimulus: There are three basic stages to writing a research paper. First, gather and read sources relevant to your topic. ______blank develop a thesis statement that captures the main argument of your paper. Third, draft the paper using evidence from your sources to support that thesis.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "Second" logically signals that the activity described in this sentence—developing a thesis statement—is the next step in the three-part sequence of stages described in the text (signaled by "First" and "Third").

Choice A is incorrect because "therefore" illogically signals consequence; the thesis is the next step in the sequence. Choice B is incorrect because "for example" illogically signals exemplification; the thesis is a distinct step. Choice D is incorrect because "instead" illogically signals an alternative; the thesis is the next step.
